author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXXVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …But to-day we have sung, “By the waters of Babylon we sat down and wept, when we remembered Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe “the waters of Babylon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The waters of Babylon” are all things which here are loved, and pass away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One man, for example, loveth to practise husbandry, to grow rich thereby, to employ his mind therein, thence to gain pleasure: let him observe the issue, and see that what he hath loved is not a foundation of Jerusalem, but a stream of Babylon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another saith, It is a grand thing to be a soldier: all husbandmen fear those who are soldiers.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But then other citizens of the holy Jerusalem, understanding their captivity, mark how the natural wishes and the various lusts of men hurry and drag them hither and thither, and drive them into the sea; they see this, and they throw not themselves into the waters of Babylon, but “sit down and weep,” either for those who are being carried away by them, or themselves whose deserts have placed them in Babylon, but sitting, that is, humbling themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O holy Sion, where all stands firm and nothing flows!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath thrown us headlong into this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why have we left thy Founder and thy society?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, placed where all things are flowing and gliding away, scarce one, if he can grasp the tree, shall be snatched from the stream and escape.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Humbling ourselves then in our captivity, let us “sit by the waters of Babylon,” let us not dare to plunge ourselves in those streams, nor to be proud and lifted up in the evil and sadness of our captivity, but let us sit, and so weep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us sit “by” the waters, not beneath the waters, of Babylon; such be our humility, that it overwhelm us not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sit “by” the waters, not “in” the waters, not “under” the waters; but yet sit, in humble fashion, talk not as thou wouldest in Jerusalem.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many weep with the weeping of Babylon, because they rejoice also with the joy of Babylon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When men rejoice at gains and weep at losses, both are of Babylon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou oughtest to weep, but in the remembrance of Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou weepest in the remembrance of Sion, thou oughtest to weep even when it is well with thee in Babylon.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “On the willows in the midst thereof we hung up our instruments of music” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The citizens of Jerusalem have their “instruments of music,” God’s Scriptures, God’s commands, God’s promises, meditation on the life to come; but while they are dwelling “in Babylon,” they “hang up their instruments.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Willows are unfruitful trees, and here so placed, that no good whatever can be understood of them: elsewhere perhaps there may.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here understand barren trees, growing by the waters of Babylon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These trees are watered by the waters of Babylon, and bring forth no fruit; just as there are men greedy, covetous, barren in good works, citizens of Babylon in such wise, that they are even trees of that region; they are fed there by these pleasures of transitory things, as though watered by “the waters of Babylon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seekest fruit of them, and nowhere findest it.…Therefore by deferring to apply the Scriptures to them, “we hang up our instruments of music upon the willows.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we hold them not worthy to carry our instruments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We do not therefore insert our instruments into them and bind them to them, but defer to use them, and so hang them up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the willows are the unfruitful trees of Babylon, fed by temporal pleasures, as by the “waters of Babylon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For there they that led us captive demanded of us words of songs, and they that led us away, an hymn” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They demanded of us words of songs and an hymn, who led us captive.…We are tempted by the delights of earthly things, and we struggle daily with the suggestions of unlawful pleasures; scarce do we breathe freely even in prayer: we understand that we are captives.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who led us captive? what men? what race? what king?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we are redeemed, we once were captives.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath redeemed us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From whom hath He redeemed us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil then and his angels led us captive: and they would not lead us, unless we consented.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Those” then “who have led us captive,” the devil and his angels, when have they spoken unto us: “Sing us one of the songs of Sion”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What answer we?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Babylon beareth thee, Babylon containeth thee, Babylon nourisheth thee, Babylon speaks by thy mouth, thou knowest not to take in save what glitters for the present, thou knowest not how to meditate on things of eternity, thou takest not in what thou askest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How shall we sing the Lord’s song in a strange land?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly, brethren, so it is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Begin to wish to preach the truth in such measure as ye know it, and see how needful it is for you to endure such mockers, persecutors of the truth, full of falsehood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reply to them, when they ask of you what they cannot take in, and say in full confidence of your holy song, “How shall we sing the Lord’s song in a strange land!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But take heed how thou dwellest among them, O people of God, O body of Christ, O high-born band of wanderers (for thy home is not here, but elsewhere), lest when thou lovest them, strivest for their friendship, and fearest to displease such men, Babylon begin to delight thee and thou forget Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fear then of this, see what the Psalmist subjoins, see what follows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I forget thee, O Jerusalem” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5), amid the speeches of those who hold me captive, amid the speeches of treacherous men, amid the speeches of men who ask with ill intent, asking, yet unwilling to learn.…What then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let my tongue cleave to my jaws, if I remember not thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, let me be dumb, he saith, if I remember not thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what word, what sound doth he utter, who uttereth not songs of Sion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is our tongue, the song of Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The song of the love of this world is a strange tongue, a barbarous tongue, which we have learnt in our captivity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dumb then will he be to God, who forgetteth Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it is not enough to remember: for her enemies too remember her, desiring to overthrow her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What is that city?” say they; “who are the Christians? what sort of men are the Christians? would they were not Christians.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the captive band hath conquered its capturers; still they murmur, and rage, and desire to slay the holy city that dwells as a stranger among them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not enough then is it to remember: take heed how thou rememberest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For some things we remember in hate, some in love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And so, when he had said, “If I forget thee, O Jerusalem,” etc., he added at once, “if I prefer not Jerusalem in the height of my joy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is the height of joy where we enjoy God, where we are safe of united brotherhood, and the union of citizenship.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There no tempter shall assail us, no one be able so much as to urge us on to any allurement: there nought will delight us but good: there all want will die, there perfect bliss will dawn on us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then he turneth to God in prayer against the enemies of that city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember, O Lord, the children of Edom” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Edom is the same who is also called Esau: for ye heard just now the words of the Apostle read, “Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated.”…Esau then signifieth all the carnal, Jacob all the spiritual.…All carnal persons are enemies to spiritual persons, for all such, desiring present things, persecute those whom they see to long for things eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against these the Psalmist, looking back to Jerusalem, and beseeching God that he may be delivered from captivity, saith—what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember, O Lord, the children of Edom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deliver us from carnal men, from those who imitate Esau, who are elder brethren, yet enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were first-born, but the last-born have won the pre-eminence, for the lust of the flesh hath cast down the former, the contempt of lust hath lifted up the latter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The other live, and envy, and persecute.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the day of Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The day of Jerusalem, wherein it was tried, wherein it was held captive, or the day of Jerusalem’s happiness, wherein it is freed, wherein it reaches its goal, wherein it is made partaker of eternity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember,” saith he, “O Lord,” forget not those “who said, Rase it, rase it, even to the foundation thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remember then, it means, that day wherein they willed to overthrow Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how great persecutions hath the Church suffered!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How did the children of Edom, that is, carnal men, servants of the devil and his angels, who worshipped stocks and stones, and followed the lusts of the flesh, how did they say, “Extirpate the Christians, destroy the Christians, let not one remain, overthrow them even to the foundation!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have not these things been said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when they were said, the persecutors were rejected, the martyrs crowned.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then he turneth himself to her, “O daughter of Babylon, unhappy;” unhappy in thy very exulting, thy presumption, thine enmity; “unhappy daughter of Babylon!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The city is called both Babylon, and daughter of Babylon: just as they speak of “Jerusalem” and “the daughter of Jerusalem,” “Sion” and “the daughter of Sion,” “the Church” and “the daughter of the Church.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As it succeedeth the other, it is called “daughter;” as it is preferred before the other, it is called “mother.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was a former Babylon; did the people remain in it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it succeedeth to Babylon, it is called daughter of Babylon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O daughter of Babylon, “unhappy” thou!…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Happy shall he be that repayeth thee, as thou hast served us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What repayment meaneth he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Herewith the Psalm closeth, “Happy, that taketh and dasheth thy little ones against the rock” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Her he calleth unhappy, but him happy who payeth her as she hath served us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we ask, what reward?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the repayment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what hath that Babylon done to us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have already sung in another Psalm, “The words of the wicked have prevailed against us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when we were born, the confusion of this world found us, and choked us while yet infants with the empty notions of divers errors.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The infant that is born destined to be a citizen of Jerusalem, and in God’s predestination already a citizen, but meanwhile a prisoner for a time, when learneth he to love ought, save what his parents have whispered into his ears?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They teach him and train him in avarice, robbery, daily lying, the worship of divers idols and devils, the unlawful remedies of enchantments and amulets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall one yet an infant do, a tender soul, observing what its elders do, save follow that which it seeth them doing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Babylon then has persecuted us when little, but God hath given us when grown up knowledge of ourselves, that we should not follow the errors of our parents.…How shall they repay her?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As she hath served us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let her little ones be choked in turn: yea let her little ones in turn be dashed, and die.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the little ones of Babylon?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evil desires at their birth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are, who have to fight with inveterate lusts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When lust is born, before evil habit giveth it strength against thee, when lust is little, by no means let it gain the strength of evil habit; when it is little, dash it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou fearest, lest though dashed it die not; “Dash it against the Rock; and that Rock is Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, let not your instruments of music rest in your work: sing one to another songs of Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Readily have ye heard; the more readily do what ye have heard, if ye wish not to be willows of Babylon fed by its streams, and bringing no fruit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But sigh for the everlasting Jerusalem: whither your hope goeth before, let your life follow; there we shall be with Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ now is our Head; now He ruleth us from above; in that city He will fold us to Himself; we shall be equal to the Angels of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We should not dare to imagine this of ourselves, did not the Truth promise it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then desire, brethren, this day and night think on.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Howsoever the world shine happily on you, presume not, parley not willingly with your lusts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it a grown-up enemy? let it be slain upon the Rock.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it a little enemy? let it be dashed against the Rock.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Slay the grown-up ones on the Rock, and dash the little ones against the Rock.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Rock conquer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be built upon the Rock, if ye desire not to be swept away either by the stream, or the winds, or the rain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If ye wish to be armed against temptations in this world, let longing for the everlasting Jerusalem grow and be strengthened in your hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Your captivity will pass away, your happiness will come; the last enemy shall be destroyed, and we shall triumph with our King, without death.
